title: Black Forest Cake

steps.text: Add cake mix, cocoa, water, oil & eggs to electric mixer bowl.

Cool in pans for 15 mins, remove from pans & continue to cool.

Unwrap & put cake layer on flat plate. Put about ½ of cherry filling on layer & leave about 1" outer edge without filling.
Place 2nd layer on top. Frost sides of cake with chocolate frosting.
Whip cream, with teaspoon put whipped cream in mounds around edge of cake.
Pour remaining cherry filling in center. Refrigerate.

steps.text: Empty contents of tea bags into a 1 quart preserving jar. Add water and stir briskly. Screw jar lid down tight so jar will not leak when turned upside down.

title: Piña Colada Cake
ingredients: 8 oz. can of crushed pineapple, packed in its own juice
18½ oz. box pineapple flavored cake mix (not pudding added type)
Rum Sauce: 3 tablespoons pineapple juice (reserved from cake)
Rum Sauce: 4 tablespoons cream of coconut

steps.text: Drain pineapple, reserving juice. Mix pineapple with coconut and set aside.
Grease and flour a 12 cup bundt pan and preheat oven to 350.
Combine cake mix, pudding, eggs, oil, rum, and cream of coconut in a large mixing bowl and beat for 2 minutes with electric mixer at high speed. Fold in pineapple/coconut mixture.
Pour into prepared pan and bake for 1½ to 1¾ hours, checking occasionally from the hour mark on. Cake is done when it is nicely browned and a testing pick comes out clean.

While cake bakes, prepare rum sauce: in a small saucepan, combine butter, sugar and pineapple juice. Heat over low flame until butter melts and sugar dissolves. Bring to boil and boil for one minute. Remove from heat and let cool about 10 minutes. Stir in rum and cream of coconut.
When cake is done, remove from oven, let cool only a short time, and punch holes in surface with fork or testing pick. Drizzle half of rum sauce over cake.

Let cake cool in pan for another 15 minutes. Turn out on serving plate. Punch holes in top and upper sides and spoon remaining sauce over cake. Garnish with extra flaked coconut if you wish. Let cool completely before cutting.

kidneySafe.note: Sodium and phosphates are the two to keep low here. Higher in sodium: packaged mixes — use unsalted / low-sodium, or less. Higher in phosphates: dairy, packaged mixes — limit these, and favor phosphate-free baking powder & fresh (not processed) foods.

title: Cherry Chocolate Top

Spread mixture over cake immediately when it comes from the oven (heat from oven cooks the egg).

notes: Great on chocolate or devil's food cake baked in a brownie pan, cut in squares and left in the pan until serving. Makes enough for one cake.

kidneySafe.note: Sodium and phosphates are the two to keep low here. Cut the added salt — season with herbs, pepper, lemon, and garlic/onion powder. Skip “lite salt” substitutes (they're potassium). Higher in phosphates: nuts, cocoa — limit these, and favor phosphate-free baking powder & fresh (not processed) foods.

ingredients.2: very light sprinkle salt → reduce or leave out; season with herbs, pepper, lemon & garlic/onion powder

steps.text: Mix margarine, sugars, oats, and vanilla. Press into an 8 inch square greased baking pan.
Bake at 350 for 10 minutes.
Melt chocolate chips and peanut butter over boiling water or in the microwave. Spread over baked bars; cut while warm and refrigerate.

kidneySafe.note: Sodium and phosphates are the two to keep low here. Higher in sodium: packaged mixes — use unsalted / low-sodium, or less. Higher in phosphates: peanuts, chocolate, oats, packaged mixes — limit these, and favor phosphate-free baking powder & fresh (not processed) foods.

title: Truffles
ingredients: 2½ lbs. bitter-sweet chocolate, chopped into small pieces

steps.text: Simmer cream in saucepan until it boils. Remove from heat; add chocolate. Whisk in butter.
Divide batch and add different liqueurs for flavor.
Chill. Form into balls. Freeze. Dip in chocolate glaze. You can roll in powdered cocoa before freezing if you don't glaze.

kidneySafe.note: Sodium and phosphates are the two to keep low here. Higher in phosphates: dairy, chocolate — limit these, and favor phosphate-free baking powder & fresh (not processed) foods.
